source_jobs.title: Founding Product Deployment Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Valence has built the only AI native coaching platform for enterprise, offering personalized, expert, and human-like guidance and support to any leader or employee. We’re not just talking about the future of work — we’re building it now, with the most innovative Fortune 500 companies across healthcare, financial services, manufacturing, and technology.



Learn more about our work with Microsoft: Valence Brings AI Coaching More Deeply Into the Flow of Work with Microsoft 365 https://www.valence.co/blog/valence-microsoft-365-may-2026

 

Our focus is on the problems that actually decide whether AI changes how organizations operate — the ones with no playbook, no obvious answers, and no guarantee of success. If you want to be part of the small group that defines how AI transforms the future at a global scale, this is your chance.

 

And this isn’t for everyone. We’re not looking for people who want predictability or incremental progress. We only want those who are restless at the edge of what’s possible, who get bored when things feel “done,” and who are driven to redefine what AI can mean for leaders, companies, and the world. Because at Valence, the work worth doing is the kind that redefines work itself.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This role owns the operational system behind successful product launches at Valence - getting capabilities turned on for clients at scale. You'll build the repeatable process that helps us introduce new features in a coordinated, scalable way across product, engineering, customer success, and our enterprise clients.

In the near term, this means rolling up your sleeves with our first cohort of priority clients - getting on the phone, working through IT and security blockers, and driving direct activation. Over time, it means building the playbook, internal enablement, and adoption infrastructure that lets us scale launches across 80+ enterprise clients without it depending on any one person. You'll be the connective tissue between Product/Engineering (what's shipping) and CS/Commercial (what clients need to hear and do).

This role will report to our VP of Engineering, but will work very collaborating across different teams.




ABOUT VALENCE

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

 

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

 

Working at Valence means you'll work directly with Fortune 500 technology leaders, building expertise through the most complex enterprise deployments while gaining insight into diverse organizational approaches to AI transformation. These aren't just any enterprise clients - they're the companies defining what AI-first business transformation looks like across every major industry.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

Drive direct activation with priority clients

 - Partner hands-on with our first cohort of clients to get new features turned on — making the calls, running the meetings, working through IT/security/legal blockers

 - Build trusting relationships with both senior sponsors (CHROs, SVPs) and day-to-day operators (L&D leads, program managers) that translate into faster activation

 - Translate new features into client-relevant value, not just product specs

Build and own the deployment system

 - Design and run a repeatable process for launching new product capabilities ac

And this isn’t for everyone. We’re not looking for people who want predictability or incremental progress. We only want those who are restless at the edge of what’s possible, who get bored when things feel “done,” and who are driven to redefine what AI can mean for leaders, companies, and the world. Because at Valence, the work worth doing is the kind that redefines work itself.

 

The Role

We are seeking a seasoned Application Security Engineer to help us secure our products and platform that serve our Fortune 500 customers. In this pivotal role, you will be working in close partnership with our engineering teams, ensuring that everything they build holds up to our security standards. While your primary focus will be application security, you are joining a small, elite team, and as such you will also work cross-domain, including working with MDM, or talking with customers directly. 

 

About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

 

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

 

Working at Valence means you'll work directly with Fortune 500 technology leaders, building expertise through the most complex enterprise deployments while gaining insight into diverse organizational approaches to AI transformation. These aren't just any enterprise clients - they're the companies defining what AI-first business transformation looks like across every major industry.



What You'll Do

 - Stay ahead of engineering: review code, architecture, design, anything that’s being considered for release

 - Guide features and designs toward security

 - Provide hands-on manual pentesting on relevant code changes

 - Build and maintain security tooling to ensure security scales with organisation

 - Advise the DevOps team on infrastructure security best practices

 - Help out with client-facing security needs, such as client calls



What We're Looking For

 - Proven experience securing products & platforms, collaborating with development teams

 - Strong expertise in application security: OWASP Top 10, threat modeling, code reviews, architecture design

 - Proficiency with AI tooling, as part of your day-to-day activities

 - Solid understanding of AI itself, including AI threats, adversarial testing 

 - Familiarity with AWS and infrastructure security overall

 - Be comfortable writing and reviewing Python and TypeScript, with other coding experience a plus

 - Experience with in

source_jobs.title: Customer Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

Learn more about our work with Microsoft: Valence Brings AI Coaching More Deeply Into the Flow of Work with Microsoft 365 https://www.valence.co/blog/valence-microsoft-365-may-2026

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

Working at Valence means building the team behind one of the fastest-growing companies in enterprise AI. You'll recruit across GTM and technical roles for a business trusted by the likes of Coca-Cola, Delta, and Bristol Myers Squibb; partnering directly with leadership, owning your process end-to-end, and shaping who gets to define what AI-first transformation looks like. The bar is high, and you'll be the one who holds it.

The Role

In this role, you'll turn Valence's Fortune 500 install base into our most powerful growth asset. Companies like Coca-Cola, Nestlé, Vanguard, UPS, and Home Depot are deploying Nadia at scale, and their stories represent some of the strongest proof points in the market. You get to be the one to tell their story.

You'll own the customer advocacy engine: developing case studies alongside senior enterprise executives, building and managing client speaking programs, and delivering sharp, sales-ready proof points that translate customer momentum into pipeline for the commercial team.

This is a high-impact role on a lean marketing team operating at startup velocity. We're looking for someone who's equal parts storyteller and operator, allergic to loose ends, and energized by working directly with the customers reshaping how work gets done in the AI era.

What You'll Do

 - Produce case studies, video testimonials, and live storytelling opportunities that showcase success stories from our Fortune 500 clients.

 - Partner closely with commercial and client-facing teams to surface customer voices for press, analyst, and content moments.

 - Translate messaging and positioning priorities into sharp customer storytelling.

 - Equip the commercial team with customer proof points by use case, industry, and persona that the team can mix and match across assets.

 - Prep and orient customers with speaking opportunities across our in-person and virtual event program.

 - Develop the programming for executive roundtables and dinners that deepen our most strategic relationships.

 - Develop a peer-to-peer community function over time, curating connections between Nadia champions across the install base.

What We're Looking For

 - 5+ years in customer marketing, advocacy, lifecycle, or product marketing. A portfolio of published case studies, video testimonials, or reference programs you've personally built carries real weight.

 - Natural commercial instinct. You think in terms of outcomes and relationships, not just checklists.

 - Sharp storyteller. You can spot which customer stories are worth investing in based on narrative potential and turn raw material into proof points that move deals.

 - Strong copywriter. You can write case studies and headlines for C-suite and CHRO audiences without sounding generic.

 - Proven executive presence. You’ve built and sustained trusted relationships with VP, C-suite, and senior leaders at large, complex enterprises.

 - Connects dots others miss, between what sales is hearing, what cust

As a Principal Software Engineer, Product, you will be instrumental in architecting, building, and scaling both backend systems and user-facing experiences that power our LLM-driven coaching platform. You will ship features end-to-end - from designing resilient APIs and integrating models to delivering polished interfaces in the browser - while collaborating closely with cross-functional partners in product, design, and infrastructure. This is a ground-floor opportunity to shape a category-defining product used by enterprise customers worldwide.

 

You’ll contribute to:

- Full stack architecture, development, and optimization of features spanning backend, AI/model integration, and frontend interfaces.

- Designing for scale, performance, and security in enterprise environments.

- Debugging across the stack - from model behavior and backend services to UI logic and client performance.

- Collaborating with product, design, and operations to deliver delightful, resilient user experiences.

- Helping evolve the engineering culture, tooling, hiring, and technical roadmap as the team grows.

What You'll Do

 - Build and extend critical backend services: architect APIs, integrate LLMs and related services, and optimize server-side systems for performance and reliability.

 - Develop polished frontend experiences: craft intuitive UI flows, ensure accessibility and responsiveness, and manage data flow between frontend and backend.

source_jobs.title: Marketing Designer
source_jobs.description:
About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

Learn more about our work with Microsoft: Valence Brings AI Coaching More Deeply Into the Flow of Work with Microsoft 365 https://www.valence.co/blog/valence-microsoft-365-may-2026

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

Working at Valence means building the team behind one of the fastest-growing companies in enterprise AI. You'll recruit across GTM and technical roles for a business trusted by the likes of Coca-Cola, Delta, and Bristol Myers Squibb; partnering directly with leadership, owning your process end-to-end, and shaping who gets to define what AI-first transformation looks like. The bar is high, and you'll be the one who holds it.

The Role

This is the first dedicated designer on our marketing team who will have a chance to own how Valence and Nadia (our AI Coach) show up across every marketing surface, and to build and extend the creative foundation for a category-defining brand.

You'll sit on the Growth team and partner daily with our marketing and events leadership, as well with the broader commercial org. The work is multidisciplinary: one day you're designing social graphics, the next a website wireframe, an event booth, a sales one-pager, or polishing client-facing materials. We move fast, bring enterprise-level sophistication to everything we put in front of the world's largest companies, and treat AI as a teammate — so we're looking for someone who designs with the same instincts.

You'll have real ownership early, and the momentum of an AI-first company behind you.

What You'll Do

 - Design campaign creative across channels: paid and organic social, email, paid media, and landing pages.

 - Build website mock-ups and wireframes, and help bring them to life.

 - Create event collateral: booth design, signage, and on-site assets for summits, salons, and partner events.

 - Produce and polish external and client-facing assets including one-pagers, sales enablement materials, and key presentations.

 - Keep Valence and Nadia (our AI Coach) visually consistent by extending and maintaining our brand system, templates, and guidelines.

 - Use AI tools to move faster - prototyping concepts in leading models, generating and refining imagery, and shipping creative experiments.

 - Partner across marketing, sales, and product to turn strategy and proof points into beautiful, enterprise-ready creative that cements Valence’s leading position in the market.

What We're Looking For

 - 1–3 years of design experience (or a portfolio that clearly demonstrates the range below); bonus-points for in-house at a software/SaaS company or agency-side serving B2B clients.

 - A portfolio or work samples are required with your application.

 - Exceptional in Figma — comfortable with components, prototyping, and design systems.

 - Hands-on experience designing in Google Slides (and/or PowerPoint) for polished, on-brand presentations.

 - AI-forward: you actively use generative AI in your workflow. You are comfortable prototyping and know the latest image-generation tools — or you can demonstrably learn fast.

 - Range across surfaces — social, web, print/events, and presentations — and the judgment to balance many projects without dropping quality.

 - Strong col

Because at Valence, the work worth doing is the kind that redefines work itself.

 

The Role

This role will lead enterprise solutions for Nadia, our AI coach, by adapting and delivering tailored implementations for complex customer environments. You will design, implement, and evaluate high-impact workflows (prompts, memory and knowledge patterns, coaching flows), balance bespoke and reusable architectures, and build the tooling and evaluation discipline that let us scale enterprise AI deployments. This role sits at the heart of Valence. You will collaborate closely with Product, AI Research/Applied AI, and Engineering to ensure our most advanced enterprise AI systems are safe, performant, and aligned with real user outcomes. This is a hybrid role that combines product sense, technical grounding, and strong delivery instinct. It is ideal for someone who loves to build, ship, and iterate.

 

About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

 

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, ServiceNow, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

 

Working at Valence means you'll work directly with Fortune 500 technology leaders, building expertise through the most complex enterprise deployments while gaining insight into diverse organizational approaches to AI transformation. These aren't just any enterprise clients - they're the companies defining what AI-first business transformation looks like across every major industry.



What You'll Do

 - Own end-to-end AI solutions: Lead the full lifecycle for enterprise-grade workflows, from discovery and design through implementation, evaluation, and iteration.

 - Architect for reuse: Identify repeatable patterns, modularize components, and define when to productize; communicate tradeoffs across speed, quality, and maintainability.

 - Drive quality and evaluation: Define prompting standards, guardrails, and success metrics (accuracy, tone, safety, outcome quality). Build lightweight evaluation frameworks that scale across clients.

 - Make hard tradeoffs: Balance speed vs. scalability; protect the platform from over-engineering while ensuring customers see real value quickly.

 - Collabor

Because at Valence, the work worth doing is the kind that redefines work itself.

 

The Role

As a founding Applied AI Engineer at Valence, you will help define and build the future of AI-powered leadership coaching, working directly with our Head of AI and cross-functional product and engineering teams. You’ll be at the intersection of generative AI research and product execution - helping design, build, and refine intelligent systems that deliver context-aware, personalized coaching at enterprise scale. This role is purposefully broad and adaptable: we don’t expect any one candidate to check every technical box, but we do look for engineers who are eager to learn, iterate rapidly, and contribute meaningfully across a range of challenges from model behavior to production systems.

 

You’ll tackle real-world AI problems - from transforming enterprise data into actionable context, to optimizing conversational experiences, to shaping how AI engages with users in meaningful and responsible ways. Your work will directly impact how our platform performs in high-stakes enterprise deployments and how leaders around the world grow through AI-facilitated insights.

 

About Valence

What You'll Do

 - Architect and build enterprise-grade AI and conversational systems that power coaching workflows and user experiences.

 - Develop, evaluate, and refine LLM-based components - balancing performance, scalability, and reliability in real use cases.

 - Integrate and manage diverse sources of structured and unstructured data to improve contextual understanding and output quality.

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Valence has built the only AI native coaching platform for enterprise, offering personalized, expert, and human-like guidance and support to any leader or employee. We’re not just talking about the future of work — we’re building it now, with the most innovative Fortune 500 companies across healthcare, financial services, manufacturing, and technology.

Learn more about our work with Microsoft: Valence Brings AI Coaching More Deeply Into the Flow of Work with Microsoft 365 https://www.valence.co/blog/valence-microsoft-365-may-2026

 

Our focus is on the problems that actually decide whether AI changes how organizations operate — the ones with no playbook, no obvious answers, and no guarantee of success. If you want to be part of the small group that defines how AI transforms the future at a global scale, this is your chance.

 

And this isn’t for everyone. We’re not looking for people who want predictability or incremental progress. We only want those who are restless at the edge of what’s possible, who get bored when things feel “done,” and who are driven to redefine what AI can mean for leaders, companies, and the world. Because at Valence, the work worth doing is the kind that redefines work itself.

 

About the Role

Valence is looking for a Product Designer to join our small, high-velocity product-design-engineering team. You'll work across the full product — from AI coaching flows to team collaboration features — shipping polished, thoughtful experiences on a weekly cadence. This isn't a role where design is handed requirements and asked to execute. You'll be embedded with product and engineering, contributing to how we think about problems, not just how we solve them visually.

You're a few years into your career, have shipped real product, and are hungry to grow in an environment that will stretch you.

What You'll Do

 - Design end-to-end features across Valence's core product — coaching flows, onboarding, dashboards, and team tooling — from concept through shipped

 - Contribute to and maintain our design system, ensuring consistency and craft as the product scales

 - Work closely with product and engineering in weekly cycles, translating requirements into clear interaction flows and high-fidelity designs

 - Participate actively in design critiques, working sessions, and paired design work with senior teammates

 - Write product copy and micro-interactions that make the experience feel polished and human

 - Explore how AI can be integrated into user experiences in ways that feel intuitive, not gimmicky

What You Bring

 - 3–5 years of product design experience with a portfolio that demonstrates strong craft and end-to-end thinking

 - Experience shipping product on a small team — you're comfortable owning a feature without heavy process or specialized support

 - Solid grounding in interaction design, visual design, and information architecture

 - Familiarity working with and contributing to a design system

 - Comfortable working in fast cycles — you can make decisions with incomplete information and iterate based on what you learn

 - Proficiency in Figma

Nice to have:

 - Experience designing conversational interfaces, chat products, or AI-powered experiences

 - Exposure to enterprise SaaS or B2B products

 - Interest in the intersection of AI, coaching, and human behavior

What Success Looks Like in 90 Days

 - You've shipped at least one end-to-end feature and have a clear sense of where your work fits in the broader product

 - You're an active contributor in design reviews and working sessions, not just a listener

 - You have a working relationship with your engineering and product partners and are embedded in the weekly rhythm of the team

Why Valence

 - Ownership & Rapid Growth

 - Outsized missions from day one, with direct responsibility for company-defining projects

 - Work alongside the executive team with transparency into strategy and decision-making

 - Influence on direction through real-

source_jobs.title: Client Partner
source_jobs.description:
Valence has built the only AI native coaching platform for enterprise, offering personalized, expert, and human-like guidance and support to any leader or employee. We’re not just talking about the future of work — we’re building it now, with the most innovative Fortune 500 companies across healthcare, financial services, manufacturing, and technology.

Learn more about our work with Microsoft: Valence Brings AI Coaching More Deeply Into the Flow of Work with Microsoft 365 https://www.valence.co/blog/valence-microsoft-365-may-2026

Because at Valence, the work worth doing is the kind that redefines work itself.

 

The Role

In this role you will work directly with senior leaders, including CHROs and CEOs at Fortune 500 companies, to help them navigate one of the hardest challenges in business today: meaningful AI adoption. This is a consultative, long-cycle commercial role focused on executive relationships, business case development, pilot design, and complex enterprise buying processes. This role sits at the intersection of enterprise sales, commercial strategy, and category creation.

 

You won’t inherit a rigid territory model or a mature playbook. You’ll help build how we win. We are intentionally reinventing the traditional enterprise sales motion by investing in deep, strategic problem solvers who can engage thoughtfully on complex organizational change.

 

You will be responsible for advancing enterprise opportunities and driving new customer growth, while partnering closely with Product, Customer Success, and Leadership to refine our go-to-market approach as the category evolves.

 

Success comes from judgment, ownership, and the ability to turn trusted executive relationships into durable enterprise partnerships. There are no individual commissions and no high-volume outbound expectations. Compensation is team-oriented - we win together, we lose together.

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

What You'll Do

 - Own and advance complex enterprise opportunities from initial engagement through close

 - Build trusted relationships with senior stakeholders, including CHROs, CEOs, and other C-suite leaders

 - Develop compelling business cases that link AI adoption

The Role

This is a rare opportunity to define what consumer-grade AI coaching experiences look like at enterprise scale. You’ll join as a senior design leader on a small, high-velocity product-design-engineering team, where your work will directly shape how millions of users interact with AI coaching every day. This role is exciting because:

 - Redefine the paradigm: Conversational and LLM-driven interfaces are still early. You’ll challenge existing patterns and establish new standards for AI-native product experiences.

 - Design as a strategic unlock: At Valence, design isn’t decoration — it’s central to trust, engagement, and adoption. Your work will directly accelerate our product and company.

 - True partnership: You’ll work alongside product and engineering leaders who deeply value design and involve it in every major decision.

 - AI-native from day one: We’re building from first principles, not layering AI onto legacy systems.

 - High performing PDE culture: We operate as a tight, collaborative team with intentional practices that make distributed work effective and energizing.

 

About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

 

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

 

Working at Valence means you'll work directly with Fortune 500 technology leaders, building expertise through the most complex enterprise deployments while gaining insight into diverse organizational approaches to AI transformation. These aren't just any enterprise clients - they're the companies defining what AI-first business transformation looks like across every major industry.



What You'll Do

 - Design features that evolve Nadia from 1:1 coaching into a multiplayer collaboration platform, including context-sharing, team dynamics, and manager tooling

 - Establish and evolve our design system to maintain craft and consistency as we scale

 - Work embedded with product and engineering to ship high-quality experiences weekly, not quarterly

 - Balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution - designing flows, refi

source_jobs.title: Client Partner
source_jobs.description:
Valence has built the only AI native coaching platform for enterprise, offering personalized, expert, and human-like guidance and support to any leader or employee. We’re not just talking about the future of work — we’re building it now, with the most innovative Fortune 500 companies across healthcare, financial services, manufacturing, and technology.

Learn more about our work with Microsoft: Valence Brings AI Coaching More Deeply Into the Flow of Work with Microsoft 365 https://www.valence.co/blog/valence-microsoft-365-may-2026

 

Our focus is on the problems that actually decide whether AI changes how organizations operate — the ones with no playbook, no obvious answers, and no guarantee of success. If you want to be part of the small group that defines how AI transforms the future at a global scale, this is your chance.

 

And this isn’t for everyone. We’re not looking for people who want predictability or incremental progress. We only want those who are restless at the edge of what’s possible, who get bored when things feel “done,” and who are driven to redefine what AI can mean for leaders, companies, and the world. Because at Valence, the work worth doing is the kind that redefines work itself.

 

The Role

In this role you will work directly with senior leaders, including CHROs and CEOs at Fortune 500 companies, to help them navigate one of the hardest challenges in business today: meaningful AI adoption. This is a consultative, long-cycle commercial role focused on executive relationships, business case development, pilot design, and complex enterprise buying processes. This role sits at the intersection of enterprise sales, commercial strategy, and category creation.

 

You won’t inherit a rigid territory model or a mature playbook. You’ll help build how we win. We are intentionally reinventing the traditional enterprise sales motion by investing in deep, strategic problem solvers who can engage thoughtfully on complex organizational change.

 

You will be responsible for advancing enterprise opportunities and driving new customer growth, while partnering closely with Product, Customer Success, and Leadership to refine our go-to-market approach as the category evolves.

 

Success comes from judgment, ownership, and the ability to turn trusted executive relationships into durable enterprise partnerships. There are no individual commissions and no high-volume outbound expectations. Compensation is team-oriented - we win together, we lose together.

 

About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

 

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

 

Working at Valence means you'll work directly with Fortune 500 technology leaders, building expertise through the most complex enterprise deployments while gaining insight into diverse organizational approaches to AI transformation. These aren't just any enterprise clients - they're the companies defining what AI-first business transformation looks like across every major industry.



What You'll Do

 - Own and advance complex enterprise opportunities from initial engagement through close

 - Build trusted relationships with senior stakeholders, including CHROs, CEOs, and other C-suite leaders

 - Develop compelling business cases that link AI adoption

source_jobs.title: Strategy & Operations Associate
source_jobs.description:
About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

Working at Valence means you'll work directly with Fortune 500 technology leaders, building expertise through the most complex enterprise deployments while gaining insight into diverse organizational approaches to AI transformation. These aren't just any enterprise clients - they're the companies defining what AI-first business transformation looks like across every major industry.

The Role

In this role you will work directly alongside our Managing Director, Europe - our co-lead of our Global Sales team - acting as an extension of his capacity across executive priorities, client relationships, sales operations, and strategic initiatives.

You will be a thinking partner and operator: someone who brings structure to ambiguity, executes with precision, and earns trust with senior leaders internally and at some of our largest enterprise partners. The best person in this role will grow quickly into a broader remit; this is the kind of seat that provides excellent generalist training for future leaders.

What You'll Do

 - Sales Operations: Keep the MD well-informed on pipeline health, team activity, and key commercial metrics. Identify trends, risks, and gaps in the pipeline and flag these proactively. Support oversight of key deals. Draft decks and communications.

 - Relationship Management: Maintain a structured and thoughtful engagement cadence with senior stakeholders and C-Suite at our largest enterprise customers. Coordinate outreach across events, emails, and meetings to keep relationships warm, consistent, and purposeful. Track relationship history and surface timely moments for re-engagement.

 - Partner & Adviser Management: Help manage relationships with a select group of channel partners and advisers, ensuring their activity is tracked and aligned to European growth goals.

 - Communications & External Presence: Assist with the preparation of talking points, speeches, and presentation materials for conferences, panels, and industry events.

 - Strategic Initiatives: Lead or support a range of ad hoc strategic initiatives as they arise. Act as a trusted thinking partner on business problems.

 - Executive Administration: maintain full ownership over the MD’s calendar, ensuring his time is allocated to the right priorities. Prepare sharp briefing materials and background context ahead of key meetings so every interaction lands well.

What We’re Looking For

 - 3-6+ years of professional experience in consulting and/or a fast-paced B2B startup.

 - Ability to diagnose a variety of problems, break them down, and solution in an impactful way.

 - High velocity execution skills to keep many work streams on track; natural attention to the small detail.

 - Fluidity with advanced AI tools for business.

 - Outstanding verbal and written communication skills, including the ability to adjust styles to the internal and external audiences.

 - Comfort with data manipulation and visualization to communicate key insights in a value-added way.

 - Self-motivated, adaptable, and resilient. Highly motivated and self-driven, with a strong desire to exceed expectations.

 - Willingness to embrace challenges, experiment and contin

source_jobs.title: Lead Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

Learn more about our work with Microsoft: Valence Brings AI Coaching More Deeply Into the Flow of Work with Microsoft 365 https://www.valence.co/blog/valence-microsoft-365-may-2026

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

Working at Valence means building the team behind one of the fastest-growing companies in enterprise AI. You'll recruit across GTM and technical roles for a business trusted by the likes of Coca-Cola, Delta, and Bristol Myers Squibb; partnering directly with leadership, owning your process end-to-end, and shaping who gets to define what AI-first transformation looks like. The bar is high, and you'll be the one who holds it.


THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Lead Recruiter to own and evolve how Valence hires at a critical stage of company growth. This is a high-ownership IC role reporting to the VP of People & Talent, with responsibility not just for executing searches, but for shaping hiring strategy, calibrating talent bar, and influencing how we make talent decisions across the company.

You’ll operate as a thought partner to hiring managers and leadership, helping define role scope, prioritize hiring needs, and ensure we are consistently hiring people who raise the bar. This role sits at the center of how Valence scales — with direct visibility into business priorities and the opportunity to build a recruiting system that is fast, rigorous, and aligned with how an AI-native company should operate.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own full-cycle recruiting across GTM (Sales, CS, Marketing) and technical roles as needed, from role definition through close

 - Partner with hiring managers and leadership to define role scope, calibrate level, and align on success criteria tied to business outcomes

 - Contribute to headcount planning and hiring prioritization, helping the company make informed tradeoffs on where and how to invest in talent

 - Design and run structured, high-signal interview processes; drive calibration across interviewers to ensure consistent and high-quality hiring decisions

 - Build proactive, high-quality pipelines through sourcing, referrals, and network mapping, with a focus on reaching top-quartile talent

 - Help shape Valence's employer brand and deliver a high-touch, high-integrity candidate experience that reflects Valence’s standards, regardless of outcome

 - Use funnel data (conversion rates, source quality, time-to-hire) to manage hiring performance and turn insight into action

 - Bring market intelligence (talent landscape, compensation, competitor dynamics) into hiring decisions and leadership conversations

 - Partner with leaders to turn what recruiting learns [about the talent market, comp, and competitors] into insight that sharpens decisions across the company


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 8+ years of full-cycle, in-house recruiting experience in high-growth B2B SaaS or AI environments, with exposure to both GTM and technical hiring

 - A clear, well-formed point of view on what great recruiting looks like - how to assess talent, design effective processes, and hold a high hiring bar

 - Genuine AI fluency: a real grasp on how AI changes recruiting, and a bias toward building it into the process

 - A track record of opera

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Platform
source_jobs.description:
Valence has built the only first-to-market AI native coaching platform for enterprise, offering personalized, expert, and human-like guidance and support to any leader or employee. We’re not just talking about the future of work — we’re building it now, with the most innovative Fortune 500 companies across healthcare, financial services, manufacturing, and technology.

Our focus is on the problems that actually decide whether AI changes how organizations operate — the ones with no playbook, no obvious answers, and no guarantee of success. If you want to be part of the small group that defines how AI transforms the future at a global scale, this is your chance.

We only want those who are restless at the edge of what’s possible, who get bored when things feel “done,” and who are driven to redefine what AI can mean for leaders, companies, and the world. Because at Valence, the work worth doing is the kind that redefines work itself.

 

The Role

As a Platform Engineer at Valence, you will build and operate the secure, reliable, and scalable foundation that lets every product and AI team ship Nadia innovations faster and with lower risk. Platform Engineering is Valence's force multiplier: we own the shared control planes for trust and safety, global access and routing, LLM governance, deployment automation, workload isolation, observability and SLOs, and AI-native engineering execution.

Your mandate is simple: increase shipping velocity and customer trust at the same time, by making the safe path the default path. You'll build the paved roads every team uses in production, and enforce minimum production standards through templates, CI/CD gates, scorecards, and time-bound exceptions, so every service is observable, reversible, and secure by default.

We don't expect any one candidate to check every technical box, but we do look for engineers who are eager to learn, iterate rapidly, and contribute across challenges that span reliability, security, global delivery, and developer experience.

 

About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

 

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

 

Working at Valence means you'll work directly with Fortune 500 technology leaders, building expertise through the most complex enterprise deployments while gaining insight into diverse organizational approaches to AI transformation. These aren't just any enterprise clients - they're the companies defining what AI-first business transformation looks like across every major industry.



What You'll Do

 - Build the paved roads. Design and operate shared platform primitives used by multiple teams (authorization and routing guardrails, secure logging, rollout controls, observability, queue infrastructure, and the centralized LLM gateway) so teams build on standards instead of reinventing them.

 - Make change safe by default. Own the reliability and change-safety systems that let the org move fast without breaking trust: CI/CD gates, migration safet

source_jobs.title: Associate, RevOps
source_jobs.description:
About Valence

We're the only company pioneering leadership coaching for large enterprises in an AI-first way. Our mission is to transform how the world's biggest companies approach learning and development, helping teams work better together through AI-powered personalization that adapts to individual goals and organizational culture using the latest advances in machine learning and natural language processing.

Learn more about our work with Microsoft: Valence Brings AI Coaching More Deeply Into the Flow of Work with Microsoft 365 https://www.valence.co/blog/valence-microsoft-365-may-2026

We've been featured in Harvard Business Review, TIME, World Economic Forum, Financial Times, Forbes and an Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies in America. Our clients represent the most diverse and sophisticated enterprise AI implementations globally, including Coca-Cola, Delta, Nestlé, General Mills, Schneider Electric, Deutsche Telekom, AstraZeneca, Prudential, CVS and Bristol Myers Squibb.

Working at Valence means building the team behind one of the fastest-growing companies in enterprise AI. You'll recruit across GTM and technical roles for a business trusted by the likes of Coca-Cola, Delta, and Bristol Myers Squibb; partnering directly with leadership, owning your process end-to-end, and shaping who gets to define what AI-first transformation looks like. The bar is high, and you'll be the one who holds it.


THE ROLE

This is not your typical RevOps role. As the third hire working directly with our Head of Revenue Operations, you'll architect, build, and maintain our revenue infrastructure through a period of hyper-growth — and turn that infrastructure into leverage by building AI-powered, automated GTM systems.

One half of the job is operator: designing the processes, reporting, and clean systems that let marketing, sales, and CS run fast. The other half is builder: using Claude Code, Clay, n8n, and adjacent tools to turn raw GTM data into personalized, scalable pipeline. There's no rigid roadmap here — just ambitious goals and the freedom to shape how we achieve them. We’ve seen consistent 3x revenue growth year over year, and you'll be instrumental in making it happen again.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build the revenue engine. Design and implement scalable processes to improve funnel conversion, objection handling, and pipeline management across marketing, sales, and CS. Own declarative config and reporting in Salesforce and HubSpot (objects, fields, flows, page layouts, approval processes), and keep the broader stack clean, integrated, and efficient.

 - Orchestrate AI personalization. Build and maintain AI-powered workflows for outbound, event engagement, CS expansion signals, and pipeline hygiene — within brand and voice guardrails.

 - Run enrichment in Clay. Design canvases for enrichment, ICP/fit scoring, and dedupe. Own provider waterfall logic, and ensure clean inputs and outputs across Salesforce, HubSpot, and the warehouse.

 - Be the single source of truth. Coordinate the flow of data across our systems — third-party and bespoke alike — to minimize duplicate records and give Marketing, Sales, and CS data they can trust.

 - Automate the platform. Build and operate non-Salesforce automations for routing, task creation, and signal-driven plays, with robust error handling, retries, and alerting — and help automate workflows across the company as we scale.

 - Analyze and report. Monitor key metrics, build clear reports, and surface actionable insights that drive decisions across GTM.

 - Drive demand gen and attribution. Partner with marketing on our event-based strategy by enriching, qualifying, and organizing lists of prospects and buyer signals.

 - Keep it reliable and documented. Keep automations current as systems evolve, and maintain runbooks so your builds are easy to share and onboard against.


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Own the stack. Autonomously maintain our GTM tech stack — third-party tools (Salesforce,
